name: macOS Canvas Side Panel
description: Tests the Canvas side panel: trigger 'Open in Canvas' from an HTML code block, verify the panel appears with WebView and header controls, resize via drag handle, toggle with ⌘⇧K, copy HTML to pasteboard, and close. Requires a model to be loaded so it can produce HTML output.

steps.description: Verify app is ready with chat prompt field.
Focus the prompt text field.
Type a prompt that asks the model to generate HTML code.
steps.value: Write a short HTML page with a title 'Canvas Test' and a blue paragraph. Put it in an html code block.

steps.description: Send the message.
Wait for the model to produce HTML output with the Open in Canvas button.
Verify the 'Open in Canvas' button is visible on the HTML code block.
Click 'Open in Canvas' to open the Canvas panel.
Wait for the Canvas panel to appear.
Verify the Canvas panel is visible with WebView and controls.
Verify the resize drag handle is present.
Drag the resize handle to widen the Canvas panel.
Verify the Canvas panel is still visible after resize.
Click 'Copy HTML' to copy content to pasteboard.
Wait for 'Copied' feedback indicator to appear.
Verify the system pasteboard contains HTML content.
Close the Canvas panel.
Wait for the Canvas panel to disappear.
Verify the Canvas panel is no longer visible.
Re-open Canvas by clicking 'Open in Canvas' again.
Wait for the Canvas panel to reappear.
Press ⌘⇧K to toggle the Canvas panel off.
Wait for the Canvas panel to disappear after ⌘⇧K.
Verify the Canvas panel is closed after ⌘⇧K toggle.
